blocks:
Surat al-Jinn

It is Meccan, the number of its verses is twenty-eight verses بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) قُلْ أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ أَنَّهُ اسْتَمَعَ نَفَرٌ مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَقالُوا إِنَّا سَمِعْنا قُرْآناً عَجَباً ** (1) Say, 'It has been revealed to me that a group of jinn listened [to the Qur’ān], and they said, "We have heard a wonderful Qur’ān,"' A wondrous book, distinct from the speech of people in the excellence of its composition and the precision of its meaning. (٢) يَهْدِي إِلَى الرُّشْدِ ** (2) 'guiding to rectitude. So we have believed in it, and we will not ascribe any partner to our Lord.' To the truth (الحق) and the correctness (الصواب). فَآمَنَّا بِهِ وَلَنْ نُشْرِكَ بِرَبِّنا أَحَداً ** Their story has preceded in Surat al-Ahqaf. (٣) وَأَنَّهُ تَعالى جَدُّ رَبِّنا ** (3) 'And that sublime is the majesty of our Lord: He has taken neither a consort nor a child.' It was said: meaning His greatness (عظمته), borrowed from al-Jadd (الجدّ) which is fortune (البخت). And al-Qummi said: It is something the Jinn said out of ignorance (بجهالة), and Allah was not pleased with it from them, and the meaning of jadd Rabbina (جدّ ربّنا) is the fortune (بخت) of our Lord. And in al-Tahdhib, al-Khisal, and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is only something the Jinn said out of ignorance, so Allah related it from them. And it was recited Innahu (إنّه) with a kasrah (vowel break), and likewise what follows it, except for the saying: أَنْ لَوِ اسْتَقامُوا وَأَنَّ الْمَساجِدَ مَا اتَّخَذَ صاحِبَةً وَلا وَلَداً.

(٤) وَأَنَّهُ كانَ يَقُولُ سَفِيهُنا عَلَى اللهِ شَطَطاً ** (4) and that our foolish one has been speaking outrageous lies against Allah. A saying far from the truth, exceeding the limit. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: meaning, darkness (ظلماء). (٥) وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّا أَنْ لَنْ تَقُولَ الْإِنْسُ وَالْجِنُّ عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً ** (5) and that we thought that humans and jinn would never speak a lie against Allah. [This is] an apology for their following the foolish one (السّفيه) in that matter. (٦) وَأَنَّهُ كانَ رِجالٌ مِنَ الْإِنْسِ يَعُوذُونَ بِرِجالٍ مِنَ الْجِنِ. (6) And that there were men among humans who sought refuge with men among the jinn. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse. He (peace be upon him) said: A man would go to the soothsayer (الكاهن) to whom the Devil (الشيطان) revealed [information], and he would say: "Tell your devil (شيطانك) that so-and-so has sought refuge with you." فَزادُوهُمْ رَهَقاً ** (So they increased them in wickedness). So, they increased the jinn, by seeking refuge in them, in arrogance (كبراً) and transgression (عتوّاً). And Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: meaning, loss (خسراناً). It is said that the jinn used to descend upon a group of humans and inform them of the news they had heard from the heaven before the birth of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And the people would practice soothsaying (يكهنون) based on what the jinn informed them.

(٧) وَأَنَّهُمْ ** And that mankind (الانس) ظَنُّوا كَما ظَنَنْتُمْ ** thought, just as you thought (7) And that they (mankind) thought, just as you thought, that Allah would never raise anyone.

O Jinn, or vice versa (meaning: the Jinn thought as mankind thought). **أَنْ لَنْ يَبْعَثَ اللهُ أَحَداً ** that Allah would never raise anyone.

The two verses are either from the speech of the Jinn, some of them to others, or a resumption of speech from Allah. And whoever vocalized anna (with fatḥah) in both of them made them part of what was revealed (الموحى به).

(٨) وَأَنَّا لَمَسْنَا السَّماءَ

(8) And that we touched the heaven We sought it (التمسناها), meaning we sought to reach it or sought its news. ** فَوَجَدْناها مُلِئَتْ حَرَساً ** (8) and found it filled with a strong guard حَرَساً (guard) is a collective noun (اسم جمع). ** شَدِيداً ** (8) strong Powerful (قويّاً), and they are the angels who prevent them (the jinn) from it. ** وَشُهُباً ** (8) and meteors The plural (جمع) of شهاب (meteor), which is the luminous object (المضيء) generated from fire (النّار).

(٩) وَأَنَّا كُنَّا نَقْعُدُ مِنْها مَقاعِدَ لِلسَّمْعِ ** (9) and that we used to take up positions in them to eavesdrop) [These positions were] devoid of guards and shooting stars (الشهب), suitable for observation (الترصّد) and listening (الاستماع). فَمَنْ يَسْتَمِعِ الْآنَ يَجِدْ لَهُ شِهاباً رَصَداً ** (but whoever eavesdrops now will find a flaming fire lying in wait for him.) meaning, a shooting star (شهاباً) observing him and for his sake, preventing him from listening by stoning (بالرجم). The explanation of this has already passed in Surat al-Hijr and al-Saffat.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith wherein the reason for the soothsayer's (الكاهن) information is mentioned, He said: "As for the information of the heaven (السماء), the devils (الشياطين) used to take up positions for eavesdropping (استراق السمع) at that time, and they were neither veiled nor stoned (ترجم) by the stars (بالنجوم). They were only prevented from eavesdropping (استراق السمع) so that no cause resembling the Revelation (الوحي) from the heavenly news would fall upon the earth, thereby confusing the people of the earth regarding what came to them from Allah, [which was necessary] for establishing the proof (الحجّة) and negating doubt (الشبهة). The devil (الشيطان) would eavesdrop on a single word from the heavenly news concerning what Allah brings about in His creation, and he would snatch it, then descend with it to the earth and cast it to the soothsayer (الكاهن). Then, [the soothsayer] would add words of his own, mixing the truth (الحقّ) with falsehood (الباطل). Thus, whatever the soothsayer (الكاهن) got right from the news he used to convey was what his devil (شيطانه) delivered to him from what he had heard, and whatever he erred in was from the falsehood (باطل) that he added to it. Since the devils (الشياطين) were prevented from eavesdropping (استراق السمع), soothsaying (الكهانة) has ceased."

(10) ** وَأَنَّا لا نَدْرِي أَشَرٌّ أُرِيدَ بِمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ أَمْ أَرادَ بِهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ رَشَداً ** (10) And that we do not know whether ill is intended for those who are on the earth, or whether their Lord intends good for them. (meaning) goodness (خيراً). (11) ** وَأَنَّا مِنَّا الصَّالِحُونَ وَمِنَّا دُونَ ذلِكَ كُنَّا طَرائِقَ قِدَداً ** (11) And that there are among us righteous ones, and among us those who are otherwise. We have been sects following diverse ways. (meaning) a people less than that. كُنَّا طَرائِقَ قِدَداً ** (meaning) scattered (متفرّقة), derived from قد when it is cut. Al-Qummi said: meaning upon different schools of thought (مذاهب مختلفة). (12) ** وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّا أَنْ لَنْ نُعْجِزَ اللهَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَنْ نُعْجِزَهُ هَرَباً ** (12) And that we knew that we will not frustrate Allah in the earth, nor will we frustrate Him by flight. (meaning) we knew (علمنا). أَنْ لَنْ نُعْجِزَ اللهَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (meaning) being wherever we are in it, وَلَنْ نُعْجِزَهُ هَرَباً (meaning) fleeing from it to the heaven, and we will not frustrate Him in the earth by flight if we are sought. (13) ** وَأَنَّا لَمَّا سَمِعْنَا الْهُدى آمَنَّا بِهِ فَمَنْ يُؤْمِنْ بِرَبِّهِ فَلا يَخافُ بَخْساً وَلا رَهَقاً ** (13) And that when we heard the guidance, we believed in it. So whoever believes in his Lord shall fear neither diminution nor oppression. (meaning) diminution (نقصان) in the recompense (الجزاء), nor that humiliation (ذلّة) should overwhelm him (يرهقه). Al-Qummi said: البخس is diminution (النقصان) and الرهق is torment (العذاب). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: الْهُدى ** (is) the Guardianship (الولاية). آمَنَّا ** (means) in our Master (بمولانا). So whoever believes in the Guardianship (بولاية) of his Master فَلا يَخافُ بَخْساً وَلا رَهَقاً. It was said: Is this the revelation (تنزيل)? He said: No, it is the interpretation (تأويل).

(١٤) وَأَنَّا مِنَّا الْمُسْلِمُونَ وَمِنَّا الْقاسِطُونَ ** (14) and that some of us are Muslims and some of us are deviators. Those who deviate (الجائرون) from the path of truth (طريق الحقّ). So whoever submits (فَمَنْ أَسْلَمَ)—it is they who have sought rectitude (فَأُولئِكَ تَحَرَّوْا رَشَداً). They aimed for (توخّوا) a great rectitude (رشداً عظيماً) which will lead them to the abode of reward (دار الثواب).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Meaning, those who affirmed our wilayah (بولايتنا).

(١٥) وَأَمَّا الْقاسِطُونَ فَكانُوا لِجَهَنَّمَ حَطَباً ** (15) But as for the unjust, they will be fuel for hell. Its fire is kindled by them. (١٦) وَأَنْ لَوِ اسْتَقامُوا عَلَى الطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسْقَيْناهُمْ ماءً غَدَقاً ** (16) And had they been upright on the path, We would have given them abundant water to drink, And that if they were to remain steadfast عَلَى الطَّرِيقَةِ ** (upon the path)—the exemplary way (الطريقة المثلى)—لَأَسْقَيْناهُمْ ماءً غَدَقاً ** (We would have given them abundant water to drink), We would have expanded provision upon them, and al-ghadaq (الغدق) is the abundant [water/provision].

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Its meaning is: We would have benefited them with abundant knowledge (علماً كثيراً) which they would learn from the Imams (peace be upon them).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means: لَوِ اسْتَقامُوا ** (if they were to remain steadfast) upon the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful, Ali, and the Successors (الأوصياء) from his progeny (peace be upon them), and accepted their obedience in their command and prohibition, لَأَسْقَيْناهُمْ ماءً غَدَقاً ** (We would have given them abundant water to drink), He says: We would have made their hearts drink faith (الإِيمان).

(١٧) ** لِنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ** (17) that We may test them thereby. that We may test them how they show gratitude to Him. ** وَمَنْ يُعْرِضْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِ ** And whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, Al-Qummi, narrated from Ibn 'Abbas, said: The remembrance of his Lord (ذِكْرِ رَبِّهِ) is the wilayah (ولاية) of 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام. ** يَسْلُكْهُ ** He will make him enter ** عَذاباً صَعَداً ** a mounting torment. a difficult one (شاقاً) that overcomes the tormented and overwhelms him.

(١٨) وَأَنَّ الْمَساجِدَ لِلَّهِ ** (18) And that the places of prostration belong to Allah are exclusive to Him, فَلا تَدْعُوا مَعَ اللهِ أَحَداً.** so do not invoke anyone along with Allah.

In al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He meant by the places of prostration (الْمَساجِدَ) the face, the two hands, the two knees, and the two big toes (الإبهامين).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Jawad (peace be upon them both), and in al-Qummi: the like of it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): That the places of prostration (أَنَّ الْمَساجِدَ) are the Successors (الأوصياء).

And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Rida: They are the Imams (peace be upon them).

(١٩) وَأَنَّهُ لَمَّا قامَ عَبْدُ اللهِ ** (19) And that when the servant of Allah stood up meaning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, calling upon Him, worshipping Him. Al-Qummi said: [The pronoun] is a metonymy for Allah. كادُوا ** He said: meaning Quraysh, يَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِ لِبَداً (would be upon him like a compacted mass) due to their crowding upon him, in astonishment at what they saw of his worship and what they heard of his recitation.

(٢٠) قُلْ إِنَّما أَدْعُوا رَبِّي وَلا أُشْرِكُ بِهِ أَحَداً ** (20) Say, 'I only invoke my Lord, and I do not associate anyone with Him.' So this is neither an innovation (بِدْع) nor a reprehensible act (مُنْكَر) that necessitates your consensus upon hating me and your astonishment. And the word قُلْ** (Say) was read as an imperative command to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله so that it conforms with what follows it.

(٢١) قُلْ إِنِّي لا أَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ ضَرًّا وَلا رَشَداً. (21) Say, 'Indeed I do not control for you any harm or any right direction.'

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله invited the people to the guardianship (وِلايَة) of Ali (peace be upon him). So Quraysh gathered before him and said: "O Muhammad, exempt us from this." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to them: "This belongs to Allah, it is not up to me." So they accused him and departed from his presence. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: قُلْ إِنِّي لا أَمْلِكُ (Say, 'Indeed I do not control...') the Ayah.

(٢٢) قُلْ إِنِّي لَنْ يُجِيرَنِي مِنَ اللهِ أَحَدٌ ** (22) Say, 'Indeed no one will protect me from Allah, He said: If I disobey Him. وَلَنْ أَجِدَ مِنْ دُونِهِ مُلْتَحَداً ** (and I will not find any refuge besides Him).' (متحرفا) (a turning away) and (ملتجأً) (a shelter).

(٢٣) إِلَّا بَلاغاً مِنَ اللهِ وَرِسالاتِهِ ** (23) [I have no recourse] except conveying [the message] from Allah and His messages.' It was said that this is an exception from مُلْتَحَداً ** (refuge), meaning: except for conveying (تبليغاً) مِنَ اللهِ ** His signs (آياته) and His messages (وَرِسالاتِهِ), for that is my refuge (ملتجأي). Or, it is an exception from لا أَمْلِكُ ** (I do not possess), meaning: I do not possess anything other than conveying the revelation (وحي) of Allah through His success (توفيقه) and His aid (عونه).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding: إِلَّا بَلاغاً مِنَ اللهِ وَرِسالاتِهِ ** (except conveying [the message] from Allah and His messages), [He said it was] concerning Ali (peace be upon him). He was asked: "Is this a revelation (تنزيل)?" He replied: "Yes." [Regarding] وَمَنْ يَعْصِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** (And whoever disobeys Allah and His Apostle), He said: [It is] concerning the guardianship (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). ** فَإِنَّ لَهُ نارَ جَهَنَّمَ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً.** (for him there will be the Fire of hell, to abide in it forever.)

** حَتَّى إِذا رَأَوْا ما يُوعَدُونَ فَسَيَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَضْعَفُ ناصِراً وَأَقَلُّ عَدَداً ** (24) When they see what they are promised, they will soon know who is weaker in supporters and fewer in number.

He suggested, saying that He means by that the Qā'im (peace be upon him) and his supporters.

And al-Qummi said: [It refers to] the Qā'im and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both) during the Return (الرجعة). And he also said: He means death and the Resurrection (القيامة).

(٢٥) قُلْ إِنْ أَدْرِي (25) Say, 'I do not know I do not know (ما ادري) أَقَرِيبٌ مَا تُوعَدُونَ أَمْ يَجْعَلُ لَهُ رَبِّي أَمَداً ** if what you are promised is near, or if my Lord will set a term for it.' a term (أجلاً) Al-Qummi said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله informed them of what would happen concerning the Return (الرجعة), they said: "When will this be?" Allah said: ** قُلْ ** (Say)—O Muhammad (يا محمد)— إِنْ أَدْرِي ** (I do not know), [to the end of] the Ayah.

(٢٦) عالِمُ الْغَيْبِ فَلا يُظْهِرُ ** meaning, He does not inform عَلى غَيْبِهِ أَحَداً. (26) Knower of the Unseen, He does not disclose His Unseen to anyone, (٢٧) إِلَّا مَنِ ارْتَضى مِنْ رَسُولٍ.** (27) except to an apostle He approves of.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "And Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله was among those whom He approved of."

And in al-Khara'ij, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) regarding it: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله is approved (مُرْتَضَى) by Allah, and we are the inheritors of that Messenger whom Allah informed of whatever He willed of His Unseen. Thus, we know what was and what will be until the Day of Resurrection."

** فَإِنَّهُ يَسْلُكُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ ** between the hands of the approved one (الْمُرْتَضَى) وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ رَصَداً Al-Qummi said: Allah informs His Messenger whom He approves of concerning the news that preceded him, and the news that will occur after him, such as the news of the Qā'im (peace be upon him), the Return (الرجعة), and the Resurrection (القيامة). And it was said that رَصَداً ** means guards (حرساً) from the angels who protect him from the snatching (اختطاف) of the devils and their confusions (تخاليطهم).

(٢٨) لِيَعْلَمَ أَنْ قَدْ أَبْلَغُوا ** (28) that He may know that they have delivered It was said: meaning, so that the Prophet to whom the revelation is sent may know that Gabriel (جبرئيل) and the angels descending with the revelation have delivered [it], or so that Allah may know that the Prophets have delivered [it], meaning, so that His knowledge relates to it as an existing reality. رِسالاتِ رَبِّهِمْ ** the messages of their Lord. just as they are, guarded against alteration. وَأَحاطَ بِما لَدَيْهِمْ ** He comprehends what is with them, [meaning] what is with the Messengers. وَأَحْصى كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عَدَداً ** and He has counted the number of everything. even the raindrops and the sand.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever frequently recites 'Say: It has been revealed' (قل أوحي) will not be afflicted in the life of this world by anything from the eyes of the Jinn (الجنّ), nor from their blowing (نفثهم), nor from their magic (سحرهم), nor from their plotting (كيدهم). And he will be with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, and he will say: 'O Lord, I do not desire a substitute for them, nor do I desire to seek a change from them.'"
